lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Hmm? What's that, young [man/woman/one]? What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, what can you do for me?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Hmm, well, mmm, do you have a family crest? I keep track of every Gielinor family, you know, so I might be able to find yours.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: I'm also something of an, mmm, a painter. If you've met any important persons or visited any nice places I could paint them for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you see if I have a family crest?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: What is your name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Mmm, [player name], let me see...

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: First things first, young [man/woman/one]! There is not much point in having a family crest if you cannot display it. You should train construction until you can build a wall decoration in your dining room.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: According to my records, your crest is [current family crest].

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Mmm, very well. Changing your crest will cost 5,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have to go get some.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: There are sixteen different symbols; which one would you like?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: But that legendary shield is still lost! I don't think it would be proper for you to wear its symbol.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Ah yes, the shield that you helped to retrieve. You have certainly earned the right to wear its symbol.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Ah, splendid, splendid. There is no better symbol than that of our fair land!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Hmm, have you ever even met the Dorgeshuun? I don't think you should wear their symbol until you have made contact with that lost tribe.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Ah yes, our new neighbours under Lumbridge. I hear you were the one who made contact with them, jolly good.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: When the dragon on Crandor Isle remains undefeated? I think you should prove yourself a dragon-slayer before you can wear a dragon symbol!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: I see you are a mighty dragon-slayer! You have certainly earned the right to wear a dragon symbol.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: A fairy? Fairies are rumoured to exist in a lost city somewhere. I don't think I should let you use them as a symbol until you have met them in person.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Hmm, mmm, yes, everyone likes pretty fairies.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: You do not seem to be very devoted to any god. I will not let you have a divine symbol unless you have level 70 prayer.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Guthix, god of balance! I'm a Saradominist myself, you know, but we all find meaning in our way, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Hmm, I'm not sure I like that HAM group, their beliefs are a little extreme for me. But if that's what you want.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: A horse? I know people talk about them, but I'm not at all sure they ever existed. I don't think I could let you use one as your symbol unless you can fetch me some kind of model of one.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Ah, I see you've brought a toy horse for me to see. An interesting beast. Certainly you can use that as your crest if you like, although it seems a bit strange to me.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: A Jungle Ogre, eh? Odd beast, very odd.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Our neighbours in the west? Very good, very good.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Ah, the fair land of Lumbridge and Varrock.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: You wish to represent yourself by a moneybag? I think to make that meaningful I should increase the price to 500,000 coins. Do you agree?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'll have to go and fetch the money.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: You wish to represent yourself by a money bag? I think to make that meaningful I should increase the price to 500,000 coins. Do you agree?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Thank you very much! You may now use a money-bag as your symbol.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Well we can't have just any pauper using a money-bag as a symbol, can we? You'll have to choose a different symbol.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Well we can't have just any pauper using a money-bag as a symbol, can we? You'll have to choose a different symbol.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: You do not seem to be very devoted to any god. I will not let you have a divine symbol unless you have level 70 prayer.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Ah, the great god Saradomin! May he smile on your house as you adorn it with his symbol!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: A symbol of death? You do not seem like a killer to me; perhaps some other symbol would suit you better.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Of... of course you can have a skull symbol, sir/madam!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Ah, Varrock, a fine city!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: You do not seem to be very devoted to any god. I will not let you have a divine symbol unless you have level 70 prayer.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: The god of Chaos? It is a terrible thing to worship that evil being. But if that is what you wish...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: You're welcome, my [boy/girl].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy a painting?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Would you like a portrait or an, mmm, a landscape? Or a map, maybe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A portrait please.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Mmm, well, there are a few portraits I can paint. I can only let you have one if you've got some connection with that person though. Who would you like?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: That will be, mmm, 1000 coins please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much money on me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Well, mmm, maybe some other time, mmm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A landscape please.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Mmm, well, I can paint a few places. Where have you had your adventures?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: That will be, mmm, 2000 coins please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A map please.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Mmm, yes, ah, I have painted maps of the known world on several different sizes of parchment. Which size would you like?

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: That will be, mmm, 1000 coins please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks.

lines.speaker: Sir Renitee
lines.text: Mmm, well, see you some other time maybe.
